title: June's Surival+
description: June's Survival+ (JS+) is a multiplayer-friendly modpack for the latest Minecraft version with visual upgrades, performance boosts, and quality-of-life tweaks.
body:
# June's Survival+

![    ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6aef44cb41d61966b01dcf65616e3014ce5d2a6b_0.webp)y

A streamlined Minecraft modpack focused on **performance**, **visual enhancements**, and **plug-and-play multiplayer compatibility**, built for the **latest version of Minecraft**.

## Performance  
Includes optimization mods like Sodium gameplay, better FPS, and faster loading. All mods are tested for world safety and stability.

## Visual Enhancements  
Improved lighting, clean UI, particle effects, and optional shader support. Enhances immersion without straying from the vanilla aesthetic.

## Plug-and-Play  
No setup needed—just install and play. Fully compatible with multiplayer and LAN servers.

## Familiar Yet Refined  
Keeps Minecraft's core feel intact while adding quality-of-life features like zoom, tooltips, smart inventory tools, and smoother UI interactions.

## Customizable  
Lightweight and modular—ideal for adding your own mods or adapting to server use.

## Up to Date  
Built for the latest stable version of Minecraft. Updates retain settings where possible and keep mods current.

## Fabric-Based  
Uses the Fabric mod loader for its speed, flexibility, and wide mod support.

## Community Friendly  
Feedback and contributions are welcome. Great for both casual and server-based play.

## Setup Instructions

To get the best experience, make sure to install the recommended resource packs in the following order:

![Guide](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/93cd5eed3ff898e35c4ad040fe9e136a0ea306c9.png)

Place them in your Minecraft resource packs menu exactly as shown above. This ensures proper visual layering and shader compatibility.


## FAQ  
**Q: Can I use this on servers?**  
A: Yes, it's compatible with vanilla and Fabric-friendly servers.  
**Q: What launcher do I need?**  
A: Any that supports Fabric (e.g., Prism, MultiMC, CurseForge, Lunar, Modrinth).  
**Q: Can I add my own mods?**  
A: Yes, it's designed to be extendable.
